name: PEUTZ-JEGHERS SYNDROME
definition: rare syndrome characterized by intestinal polyposis and mucocutaneous pigmentation (= 1-5-mm small elongated melanin spots on mucous membranes (lower lips, gums, palate) +

demographics.Genetics: autosomal dominant with incomplete penetrance; often spontaneous mutations
demographics.Frequency: 1÷7,000 live births; in 50% familial, in 50% sporadic; most frequent of polyposis
demographics.Path: multiple small sessile / large pedunculated polyps
demographics.Histo: benign hamartomatous (?) polyp with a smooth muscle core arising from muscularis
demographics.Location: stomach, duodenum, colon; small bowel (least common)

distribution: stomach, duodenum, colon; small bowel (least common)
discriminating_features: multiple usually broad-based polyps separated by wide areas of intervening flat mucosa
myriad of 1-2-mm nodules of up to several cm = carpet of polyps
intussusception usually confined to small bowel

general: multiple usually broad-based polyps separated by wide areas of intervening flat mucosa
myriad of 1-2-mm nodules of up to several cm = carpet of polyps
intussusception usually confined to small bowel
Stomach + duodenum: diffuse involvement with multiple polypoid lesions:

clinical.Prognosis: decreased life expectancy (risk of cancer approaching 40% by 40 years of age)
